programme.desc: Mike shocks the owner of the 'twice done house' when he discovers mold in her attic. Then, the exterior of the half done house gets a major facelift while Sherry and designer Kimm shop for quartz for the bathroom and fireplace.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: Wish Come True
programme.desc: The twice done house's troublesome fence, windows and caulking are done right for the first time, and Michael brings the homeowner over to show her the progress. Meanwhile, the half done house becomes the fully done house and the family is welcomed home.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: Holmes Is Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Work continues on the twice done house as the crew finishes the flooring and the kitchen is installed. Mike must take care of a health issue, leaving Sherry and Michael to put the finishing touches on the renovation.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Blacksmith Breakroom
programme.desc: The crew build a pioneer blacksmith shop made from reclaimed beams, barnwood and rafters to serve as their office breakroom. Mark helps set up the frame, then a local blacksmith installs the forge and gives the guys an anvil lesson.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Saving the Cabin That Saved the Company
programme.desc: Mark Bowe and his crew work to salvage a cabin that saved their business. It takes a variety of repairs to bring it back to life, but once it's up, Katherine has the best surprise of all. Later, Mark's pal Eddie makes a lamp out of reclaimed materials.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Building a Log Chapel With a Stained Glass Window
programme.desc: Mark and the guys head to Johnny Jett's hometown in Kentucky to build a log chapel. First, they reclaim a stained glass window from a deteriorating 100-year-old church. Then they build the chapel and install the window.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: A Barnwood Miracle
programme.desc: The Mt. Olivet Church has been the heart of Pocahontas County, WV for 137 years. The old log structure is struggling to survive, so Mark and the guys are called in to take it down and restore it. As the job ends, the community pulls off a surprise ending.

programme.desc: Michael surprises Ashley with an 1893 raised cottage that he purchased before seeing the full interior. The duo soon finds themselves in over their heads when they realize it's not quite the hidden gem Michael thought it was.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Dream House
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley stumble upon a pre-1900s Victorian after some curbside dumpster diving. They hit New Orleans for some perfect vintage decor then return to combine two rooms into one spacious kitchen and make this the dream house worth of the name.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Tiny Craftsman
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley revisit a house they tried to purchase a year ago and find the small 1920s craftsman has been stripped of all its original features.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Run Down Family Rental
programme.desc: Ashley and Michael take on a rundown, family-owned 1939 craftsman. The project is an emotional journey for the duo as the bland home is transformed into a breath-taking abode with room expansions and some inspired flooring choices.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: 1913 Beach Bungalow Restoration
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley find a rundown 1913 beach bungalow that's about to be demolished. They transform the small kitchen into a modern, open-concept space with vintage touches and restore the original hardwood floors and shiplap walls.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Los Angeles, California
programme.desc: Casey travels to sunny Los Angeles for some epic eats including a 2.5-pound fusion burrito loaded with every ingredient in the kitchen and drenched in hot sauce 600 times hotter than a jalapeno.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Diego, CA
programme.desc: Casey travels to San Diego to face off against a five and a half pound stack of pancakes layered with bacon, eggs, sausage, country-fried steak and thick-cut ham.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Camp Pop
programme.desc: Noah Cappe heads to northern Indiana, where the Hoosiers in Valparaiso are doing things right at the Porter County Fair. The Super Ramen Dog combines two delicious foods Noah didn't know belonged together, the Elephant Ear Taco.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Titanic Treats
programme.desc: From monster sushi rolls and a five-layer pizza cake to Texas-sized cinnamon rolls and a towering pancake sundae, these dining hot spots are bursting at the seams with titanic treats.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Sandwich Heroes
programme.desc: From fried bologna made fancy to bright lobster on pitch-black bread, these mild-mannered hometown standards go beyond the same-old-same-old and earning their bragging rights as sandwich heroes.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Sweet Indulgences

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Off the Eaten Path
programme.desc: Katie's in the know on Sullivan's Island, S.C where she is introduced to the Peanut Butter and Jelly Burger.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Cheesy Breezy
programme.desc: Katie Lee is toasting the gooey-est of dairy delights. Katie teams up with her Kitchen Co-Host, Marcela Valladolid for tacos in San Diego.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: The Gulf Coast of Mississippi
programme.desc: Katie Lee is on a mission to find the best beach food along the Gulf Coast of Mississippi.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: The Pacific Coast Highway
programme.desc: Katie Lee is hitting California's famed Pacific Coast Highway to find delicious, international beach food in a beautiful coastal setting. In Monterey, she discovers rich Italian influences in cioppino with local Dungeness crab.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Tybee Island
programme.desc: Katie Lee heads to Tybee Island, Georgia, a barrier island just 18 miles southeast of culinary Mecca Savannah. The small island is full of big flavors, and Katie starts her exploration with perfectly sweet local shrimp and a spicy blackened fish hoagie.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Open House Brunch
programme.desc: An open house style brunch includes orange spiced crumb cake and creamy scrambled eggs.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: New Diner Favorites
programme.desc: Chef Bobby Flay dresses up classic diner dishes like chocolate hazelnut s'more French toast, baked pumpkin oatmeal with a cinnamon crisp and an anything-goes style pasta scramble with breakfast sausage, eggs, kale and tomatoes.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Brunch in Bloom
programme.desc: Chef Bobby Flay prepares a garden fresh menu using his favorite fruits, vegetables and herbs like strawberry waffles with lemon curd cream, an open faced omelet with herb cream cheese topped with smoked salmon and grilled asparagus.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Breakfast in Bed
programme.desc: Chef Bobby Flay is preparing a 'breakfast in bed' style brunch. Bobby wines and dines in bed with an indulgent menu of baked eggs with prosciutto, candied tomatoes and herb parmesan cream, and chocolate covered berries.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Juicy, Crunchy and Tangy
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in Lexington, Ky. for a plump pork burger and ingenious smoked chicken wings smothered in a blackberry jalapeno sauce.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Deep-Fried Comfort Creations
programme.desc: Michael digs into an incredible one-of-a-kind creation: a deep-fried burger.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Smoked Smorgasbord For A Shark's Appetite
programme.desc: In Savannah, Ga., Michael Symon finds a food truck serving up two can't-miss creations: cheeseburger tacos with a house-made beer cheese sauce and a Jamaican jerk fried fish taco with mango salsa.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Slammin' Hot and Smoky
programme.desc: In Asheville, N.C., Michael Symon digs into a slammin' sandwich with three different cuts of pork smothered in triple-cheese sauce.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Canadian National Exhibition/ Prairiefest

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Calgary Stampede
programme.desc: At the Calgary Stampede, Noah Cappe indulges in a slice of pizza loaded with edible insects.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Some Like It Popped

programme.title: Carnival Eats: When Harry Met Salad
programme.desc: Noah Cappe's first stop is the Wayne County Fair in Richmond, Ind., where he takes a stroll with the Walking BBQ, feels the groove of the King with a bacon PB&J quesadilla, goes healthy-ish with a deep fried salad.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Ice Cream Dreams
programme.desc: Noah samples ice cream sensations from sweet to savory. Whether it's piled on top of delicious homemade donuts, or snuggling in-between bacon wafers, these ice cream dream dishes will have you begging for more.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Fusion Fiesta

programme.desc: When Night Ninja pushes the Ninjalinos too far, they become Owlette’s new sidekicks. When Greg doesn’t take care of Cameron's mini-car, it goes missing.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Hi, Jinx!/ Pete's Junkyard Helpers
programme.desc: Emmy Lou meets Jinx the cat and gets the gang to help her find the kitty a new home. The Happy Helpers run Pete's junkyard while he takes his Mama out to lunch for her birthday.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Pit Stop and Go!/ Alarm on the Farm!
programme.desc: It's the first 'Pit Stop and Go Rally' where pit crews compete against each other. The Happy Helpers look after Clara Cluck's farm while she's out of town.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Birthday Heros for Hero / Maple Cheer-Up
programme.desc: The pups must fix Hero's new birthday toy after Rolly and Keia accidentally break it. The Pugs head to Canada to save Bob's pancake surprise for Bonnie!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: One Small Ruff for Pup-kind / The Lost Bouncy Ball
programme.desc: The pups head to Space Camp to give a dog toy to Chloe; The pups get help to find their bouncy ball.

programme.title: Vampirina: Hiccupire / Uncle Bigfoot
programme.desc: When Vee gets a case of the vampire hiccups, Poppy and Bridget struggle to find a way to scare them away. Boris' old college roommate, Bigfoot, comes to stay at the Scare B&B.

programme.title: PJ Masks: May the Best Power Win/ Moonbreaker
programme.desc: Romeo creates an All-in-One belt to combat the PJ Masks' new powers.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Desert Pups/ Okavango Odyssey
programme.desc: Bob gets a flat tire on a road trip with the Pugs; The Pugs look for Bob's Moms' lost locket.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: I Heart Rufus/ Trolley Trouble
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly reunite Cupcake and Rufus after a fight; Bob forgets his camera on a trolley.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Happy Birthday, Toodles
programme.desc: Everybody wants to give Toodles a surprise birthday party -- but there's a problem. They'll need Mouseketools to prepare for the party.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Super Goof's Super Puzzle
programme.desc: In order to help his friends solve a puzzle, Goofy uses his alter-ego 'Super Goof'.

programme.title: Bluey: Bob Bilby / Spygame / Takeaway
programme.desc: Bingo brings home a Bob Bilby puppet; Bluey and Bingo play a spy game in the park; Bluey and her family have to wait for their takeaway order.

programme.title: Bluey: The Weekend / BBQ / Fruitbat
programme.desc: Bluey and her family play a game in the garden; Bingo searches for ingredients for a salad; Bluey dreams about a fruit bat.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Stop That Heist/ Lights! Camera! Help!
programme.desc: Billy Beagle's famous Golden Rumble Seat Roadster becomes the target of an infamous international thief. The Happy Helpers become personal assistants to a movie star!

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Shenannygans!
programme.desc: Mickey and the gang participate in high-spirited races around the globe.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: The Golden Boo-Boo
programme.desc: The Clubhouse pals go on an adventure, in a search for the legendary Golden-Boo-Boo statue.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Mickey's Show and Tell
programme.desc: Martian Mickey pays a visit to the Clubhouse pals and participates in a special show and tell presentation.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Pluto's Ball
programme.desc: Pluto loses his favourite ball when he's outside playing with it and bounces it too high.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Daisy's Pony Tale
programme.desc: Professor Von Drake's new Fritzer-Spritzer Spray Potion is accidentally sprayed on Daisy's ponytail, making it grow super long.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Mickey's Big Band Concert
programme.desc: The Sensational Six Polka Band is planning to provide the musical accompaniment for the baby elephants' dance performance.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Clarabelle's Clubhouse Carnival
programme.desc: Mickey and his friends put on a fun carnival.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Daisy's Grasshopper
programme.desc: Mickey and pals help Daisy find her new friend, Wilbur the Grasshopper.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Donald Hatches an Egg
programme.desc: The clubhouse pals discover a mystery egg among Goofy's rock collection; they try to find out what kind of animal is inside in order to return it to its mother. In the meantime, Donald Duck serves as a reluctant babysitter when the little egg develops.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: Goofy Baby
programme.desc: Professor Von Drake has invented a time machine and Goofy is his first volunteer.

programme.title: Doc McStuffins: Caught Blue-Handed/ To Squeak, or not to Squeak
programme.desc: When a mysterious rash of blue spots breaks out among some of the toys in the clinic, Doc traces the 'mystery pox' back to Donny's toy monster, Glo-Bo, who had been finger-painting earlier in the day.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Roadster Racers: Super-Charged: The Big Cheesy / Shenannygans!
programme.desc: During the Cooper's Hill Cheese Rolling Chase, a cheese wheel spins out of control! The Happy Helpers work as nannies for two children in London and take them on new experiences!

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: A Gollywood Wedding!/ No Dilly Dally in New Delhi!
programme.desc: Mickey and his pals travel to New Delhi, India, for the wedding of their friends Savi and Raj. The Happy Helpers assist an old friend who runs an auto-rickshaw business by being Tuk-Tuk Drivers for the day.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Hawaii Pug-Oh/ A.R.F.
programme.desc: When Bob says he'd love to feel the sand on his toes, pug puppy brothers Bingo and Rolly go on a mission to Hawaii. Then, Bob introduces the pups to A.R.F., a robotic dog he created that is programmed to clean up their messes.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The French Toast Connection/ Take Me Out to the Pug Game
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly travel to France to get Bob some French toast. Then, the pugs chase Bob's prized baseball across the city.

programme.title: Mira, Royal Detective: The Case of the Wrecked Recital / The Case of the Vanishing Vessels
programme.desc: When Prince Neel's sitar goes missing, Mira must track it down before great aunt Rupa finds out. Then, Mira goes on a mysterious underground adventure to find toy boats for a race.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Rock-A-Bye Birdie/ The Fly-Along
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy must impress Captain Beakman when she joins them for a ride-a-long delivery. When Mimi the mockingbird sings K.C.'s lullaby she puts everyone at T.O.T.S. to sleep.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: You've Gotta Be Kitten Me / Whale, Hello There
programme.desc: On Pip and Freddy’s first day, they deliver a kitten who mistakes them as her parents. When there is a two-ton baby whale delivery, the fliers compete in a fly-off for the task.

programme.title: Bluey: Early Baby / Hospital / Daddy Putdown
programme.desc: Rusty and Indy come into conflict; Dad is admitted to an imaginary hospital; Dad puts Bluey and Bingo to bed.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: A Pyramid Scheme/ Special Delivery
programme.desc: Bongo and Rolly travel to Egypt to find the Great Pyramids. Then, the pugs try to deliver a Mother's Day card for Bob.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Design-a-dog/ Ice, Ice Puggy
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly accidentally chew Bob's childhood toy. Then, when Bobo's icemaker breaks, the pugs go to Antarctica to get him ice.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The Go-long Retriever/ Pot O' Pugs
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly try to get away from a self-fetching stick that begins chasing them. Then, when Bob says he needs a pot of gold, the pups search for one at the end of a rainbow.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Hissy's Big Day/ Go, Dog. Go!
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly try to cheer Hissy up by taking her to their favourite places. Later, A.R.F. is reprogrammed to take the pugs where they want, but not being them back.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Hiccup Hazard/ The Great Robot Race
programme.desc: Pip’s hiccups threaten their delivery so he asks Freddy to do the impossible: scare them out of him. Pip and Freddy have to prove that delivery birds are better than a stork drone.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Out Foxed/ Elephant in the Room
programme.desc: Freddy's status as the best hide-and-seeker is challenged after he and Pip lose to a baby fox. Pip and Freddy help K.C. earn her first Golden Feather on Cleaning Day.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Mickey's Mixed-Up Motor Lab/ Wishy Washy Helpers
programme.desc: Mickey invents a new Motor Lab to add new upgrades to the gang's roadsters. The Happy Helpers offer their services to make Bigby Towers' windows sparkle and shine.

programme.title: PJ Masks: PJ Sky Pirates / The Disappearing Ninjas
programme.desc: When Night Ninja steals equipment from Pirate Day at school, Catboy jeopardizes the PJ Masks rescue mission. PJ Masks investigate Night Ninja when the ninjalinos keep disappearing!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Bob and Ana's Bubble Bummer/ Seen Any Seashells?
programme.desc: The Playcare float breaks down before the People and Pets Parade! The Pugs and Keia head out on an ocean-bound mission to find the perfect seashell for Ana!

programme.title: Bluey: Kids/ Butterflies/ The Creek
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey want to play a game during a shopping trip; Bluey runs away, leaving Bingo all alone; The kids and Dad go on an outing to the creek.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Starship Piggy/ My Buddy
programme.desc: Piggy takes credit for the spaceship Kermit made and takes the Muppet Babies to outer space. Animal lets Fozzie borrow his stuffed bunny, Buddy.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Wishology: The Beginning
programme.desc: Centuries ago, fairy ancestors hid a mystical wand on Earth that has the power to defeat The Darkness, but only the Chosen One depicted in the Fairy World prophecy can unlock its mysteries. Timmy gets chosen for the task, as he resembles the prophecy.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Fairly Odd Baby
programme.desc: When Timmy becomes aware of Cosmo and Wanda's desire to be parents, he wishes them up a baby. However, the Anti-Fairies and Pixies join forces to use the child to take over the world. Jorgen helps Timmy, Cosmo and Wanda save the day.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Wishology: The Exciting Middle Part
programme.desc: Timmy learns that to truly defeat The Darkness, he needs a second wand, which is located on the far side of the universe. The threat from the robotic Eliminators still remains as they have stolen Yugopotamia's Fake-i-fier technology.

programme.title: Power Rangers Beast Morphers: Game On!
programme.desc: It's not all fun and games when Devon's favourite video game is brought to life in the form of a new Robotron.

programme.title: Power Rangers Beast Morphers: Cruisin' for a Bruisin'
programme.desc: Devon discovers that Cruise has some skills that will help the Rangers in battle and pushes him to join the team.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Good Neighbors/ Skill Crane
programme.desc: Squidward obsesses over winning a stuffed animal; Squid's security system backfires.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Pest of the West
programme.desc: SpongeBob discovers that he was related to the great Western hero, SpongeBuck.

programme.desc: A freak jousting accident lands Bob and Pat in a medieval Bikini Bottom.

programme.desc: Spongebob attempts and fails his driving test again; Bob tries to join a special club.

programme.desc: Former President Barack Obama delivers a commencement message for America’s high school seniors. Joining President Obama are LeBron James, Malala Yousafzai, the Jonas Brothers, Pharrell Williams, Megan Rapinoe, H.E.R., and Ben Platt.

programme.title: Mom: Zombies and Cobb Salad
programme.desc: When Bonnie loses her job and struggles to keep her life together, Christy tries to get her back on track.

programme.title: Mom: Belgian Waffles and Bathroom Privileges
programme.desc: Christy has trouble accepting how much easier life is with Bonnie in the house. Meanwhile, Baxter sets up camp in the driveway.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Release the Dogs
programme.desc: Unable to sleep, Alan takes one of Charlie's suggestions and goes for a jog on the beach. When his jog takes a disastrous turn, he then turns to a psychiatrist in the hopes that discovering the source of his insomnia will help alleviate it.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Corey's Been Dead for An Hour
programme.desc: Charlie becomes annoyed with Alan for constantly sponging off him and promising to cover the tab 'next time.' When Alan tries to leave Charlie the cheque for dinner with two women, Charlie tries to beat him at his own game.

programme.title: Friends: The One With Rachel's Book

programme.title: Friends: The One With Phoebe's Cookies

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: Mud, Sweat, and Tears
programme.desc: A leaky ceiling brings down the house, literally, a race car makes an unscheduled pit stop through a living room wall, and 'passing wind' takes on a whole new meaning during a weather report.

programme.title: The World's Funniest Weather: It's Snow Big Deal
programme.desc: A male ballet dancer takes one to the nutcracker, a meteorologist catches a nasty bug in his mouth, and a beautiful woman dances, but only her pants do the splits.

programme.title: Wipeout: Boss and Employee: Third Shift
programme.desc: It’s the ultimate field trip away from the office as bosses and employees take a crack at the Wipeout Qualifier together as partners.

programme.title: Robert Jeffress
